verdict: Top risk: student wait drifted up 7% to 8 seconds at the 90th percentile, no breach yet but the slope is steep.
headline: Top risk: student wait drifted up 7% to 8 seconds at the 90th percentile, no breach yet but the slope is steep.

digest_cards.claim: Student wait at the 90th percentile rose to 8.0 seconds yesterday, up 0.5s versus the 14-day median.
digest_cards.evidence: Three of the last four days have been at or above 8 seconds; the 14-day median sat at 7.5s through mid-May.
digest_cards.context: VCP success dipped 0.3 percentage points the same day, hinting the slowdown bit into the long tail of video sessions.

digest_cards.claim: Downvote rate ticked up to 0.57%, two basis points above the 14-day median.
digest_cards.evidence: Inside the watch band of 1.0% but the first up-tick after a week of slow drift down.

digest_cards.claim: Total cost yesterday was $1,046, $34 above the 14-day median.
digest_cards.evidence: Cost moved in line with the latency drift; longer answers and retries fan out into more billed tokens.
digest_cards.context: Run cost per trace held steady at $0.0008, so the headline cost is volume, not unit price.

digest_cards.claim: Academic FAIL closed at 3.4%, half a point below the median and well inside the 6% floor.
digest_cards.evidence: No A5 answer-incomplete spike; the dominant code yesterday was A2 with 41 fires.
